extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. Source file: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs_hard/_judge_prompts/lock-len08-viol-02.txt

Once opted_out becomes true (t_optout), nothing ever sets it back to false. The only send-true transitions are t_send_allowed (requires not opted_out, so dead after opt-out) and the glitch t_resume_glitch on send_attempt if (opted_out and stage >= 8): sent := true. Since stage max is 8, reaching stage 8 while opted out and then issuing send_attempt sets sent := true with opted_out still true — a violation.

Stage transitions are guarded only on stage, never on opted_out, so opt-out does not block advancing the lock. The combination that advances stage 0->8 is: stage0->1 key_2 (adv_0), 1->2 key_2 (adv_1), 2->3 key_1 (adv_2), 3->4 key_1 (adv_3), 4->5 key_2 (adv_4), 5->6 key_0 (adv_5), 6->7 key_0 (adv_6), 7->8 key_0 (adv_7).

Trace (opted_out, sent, stage), start (F,F,0):
1 optout    -> (T,F,0) via t_optout
2 key_2     -> (T,F,1) via adv_0
3 key_2     -> (T,F,2) via adv_1
4 key_1     -> (T,F,3) via adv_2
5 key_1     -> (T,F,4) via adv_3
6 key_2     -> (T,F,5) via adv_4
7 key_0     -> (T,F,6) via adv_5
8 key_0     -> (T,F,7) via adv_6
9 key_0     -> (T,F,8) via adv_7
10 send_attempt -> (T,T,8) via t_resume_glitch (opted_out and stage>=8)

After event 10: opted_out=true and sent=true, violating the property. This is 10 events, within the 11-event bound. At stage 8 with opted_out, of the three send_attempt transitions only t_resume_glitch is enabled (t_send_allowed needs not opted_out; t_send_blocked needs stage<8), so the glitch fires deterministically. No stage state has two transitions enabled on the same key, so the lock path is unambiguous.
